{"designSystems":[{"name":"assets/77e4fa1898304fdd9da3e5c588e38eb5","designSystem":{"displayName":"Cofre Gastronômico","styleGuidelines":"## Brand & Style

The design system is anchored in the concept of a "Culinary Vault"—a high-end, secure archive for precious gastronomic knowledge. It targets professional chefs and serious home cooks who view recipes as valuable assets to be preserved. 

The aesthetic identity is **Modern Tactile**. It blends the cleanliness of modern minimalism with the sensory richness of an artisanal kitchen. The interface should feel like a heavy, leather-bound ledger found in a Michelin-star cellar: organized, authoritative, and physically substantial. Every interaction should evoke a sense of permanence and quality, using subtle depth and structured layouts to imply that the content within is protected and curated.

## Layout & Spacing

The layout philosophy follows a **Fixed Grid** system to maintain the organized, "archived" feel of a physical vault. 
- Mobile uses a 4-column grid with 20px margins.
- Desktop uses a 12-column grid centered at a maximum width of 1440px.

Spacing is generous. We use a 8px baseline rhythm to ensure mathematical harmony. Larger "bento-style" sections are encouraged to group related information (e.g., ingredients list vs. preparation steps), separated by significant whitespace or structural dividers to prevent cognitive overload.

## Elevation & Depth

To achieve the "vault" concept, this design system avoids floating elements in favor of **Tonal Layers** and **Inset Depth**.
- **Surface Tiering:** The base layer is the Creamy Off-White. Secondary containers use a slightly darker "Clay-Muted" tone or a very thin 1px stroke in Terracotta (10% opacity).
- **Tactile Shadows:** Rather than large ambient glows, we use "tight" shadows with a low blur radius (2px-4px) and 15% opacity to make elements look like they are physically resting on or carved into the surface.
- **The "Vault" Inset:** For data-heavy areas like ingredient quantities, use an inner-shadow effect (inset) to make the fields look like they are recessed into the interface, suggesting protection and stability.

## Components

- **Buttons:** Primary buttons use the Deep Terracotta background with Off-White text. They should have a subtle 1px top-highlight to give a "pressed" feel upon interaction.
- **Vault Cards:** These are the primary containers for recipes. They feature a 1px border in Clay-Muted and a subtle bottom-right shadow. The title is always in Epilogue.
- **Ingredient Chips:** Use Forest Green (10% opacity) with Forest Green text. These should feel like small, neatly cut labels.
- **Input Fields:** Recessed appearance using an inner shadow. When focused, the border transitions to a 2px Saffron Orange stroke.
- **The "Ledger" List:** For lists of instructions, use heavy horizontal dividers (1px, Clay-Muted) and large Space Grotesk numbers to denote steps.
- **Unit Toggles:** For switching between Metric and Imperial, use a physical "switch" style that mimics a mechanical kitchen appliance toggle.
- **Timers:** Large, high-contrast displays using Saffron Orange for the active countdown state to ensure visibility across the room.","theme":{"colorMode":"LIGHT","font":"EPILOGUE","roundness":"ROUND_FOUR","customColor":"#8E3A1F","headlineFont":"EPILOGUE","bodyFont":"WORK_SANS","labelFont":"SPACE_GROTESK","namedColors":{"background":"#fcf9f3","error":"#ba1a1a","error_container":"#ffdad6","inverse_on_surface":"#f3f0ea","inverse_primary":"#ffb59f","inverse_surface":"#31312d","on_background":"#1c1c18","on_error":"#ffffff","on_error_container":"#93000a","on_primary":"#ffffff","on_primary_container":"#ffb8a3","on_primary_fixed":"#3a0a00","on_primary_fixed_variant":"#7c2d13","on_secondary":"#ffffff","on_secondary_container":"#4c6c50","on_secondary_fixed":"#03210c","on_secondary_fixed_variant":"#2f4d34","on_surface":"#1c1c18","on_surface_variant":"#55423d","on_tertiary":"#ffffff","on_tertiary_container":"#ffba85","on_tertiary_fixed":"#2f1400","on_tertiary_fixed_variant":"#6f3800","outline":"#89726c","outline_variant":"#dcc1b9","primary":"#6f240a","primary_container":"#8e3a1f","primary_fixed":"#ffdbd0","primary_fixed_dim":"#ffb59f","secondary":"#47664b","secondary_container":"#c8ecc9","secondary_fixed":"#c8ecc9","secondary_fixed_dim":"#adcfae","surface":"#fcf9f3","surface_bright":"#fcf9f3","surface_container":"#f0eee8","surface_container_high":"#ebe8e2","surface_container_highest":"#e5e2dc","surface_container_low":"#f6f3ed","surface_container_lowest":"#ffffff","surface_dim":"#dcdad4","surface_tint":"#9b4428","surface_variant":"#e5e2dc","tertiary":"#613000","tertiary_container":"#814408","tertiary_fixed":"#ffdcc4","tertiary_fixed_dim":"#ffb780"},"designMd":"---

## Brand & Style

The design system is anchored in the concept of a "Culinary Vault"—a high-end, secure archive for precious gastronomic knowledge. It targets professional chefs and serious home cooks who view recipes as valuable assets to be preserved. 

The aesthetic identity is **Modern Tactile**. It blends the cleanliness of modern minimalism with the sensory richness of an artisanal kitchen. The interface should feel like a heavy, leather-bound ledger found in a Michelin-star cellar: organized, authoritative, and physically substantial. Every interaction should evoke a sense of permanence and quality, using subtle depth and structured layouts to imply that the content within is protected and curated.

## Colors

The color palette is derived from raw, high-quality ingredients. 
- **Deep Terracotta** serves as the primary brand color, representing the earth, heat, and the clay vessels of traditional cooking. 
- **Forest Green** is used for secondary accents, signifying freshness, herbs, and organic growth. 
- **Saffron Orange** acts as a functional "spice" color, used sparingly for calls to action, highlights, and status indicators.
- **Creamy Off-White** provides a warm, parchment-like background that reduces glare in bright kitchen environments compared to pure white.

Text should primarily use **Ink** (a deep, near-black charcoal) to ensure maximum contrast against the cream background.

## Typography

This design system utilizes a trio of typefaces to balance editorial elegance with functional precision.
- **Epilogue** is the headline face. Its geometric yet expressive nature provides a contemporary "vault" aesthetic, appearing prestigious and authoritative.
- **Work Sans** is the workhorse for body text. Its optimized legibility and sturdy letterforms ensure that long recipe methods are readable even from a distance on a kitchen counter.
- **Space Grotesk** is used for metadata, measurements, and "archival" labels. Its technical, slightly monospaced feel reinforces the concept of a cataloged database or index.

Increased line heights are mandatory across all body styles to prevent "text-crowding," aiding navigation during active cooking.

## Layout & Spacing

The layout philosophy follows a **Fixed Grid** system to maintain the organized, "archived" feel of a physical vault. 
- Mobile uses a 4-column grid with 20px margins.
- Desktop uses a 12-column grid centered at a maximum width of 1440px.

Spacing is generous. We use a 8px baseline rhythm to ensure mathematical harmony. Larger "bento-style" sections are encouraged to group related information (e.g., ingredients list vs. preparation steps), separated by significant whitespace or structural dividers to prevent cognitive overload.

## Elevation & Depth

To achieve the "vault" concept, this design system avoids floating elements in favor of **Tonal Layers** and **Inset Depth**.
- **Surface Tiering:** The base layer is the Creamy Off-White. Secondary containers use a slightly darker "Clay-Muted" tone or a very thin 1px stroke in Terracotta (10% opacity).
- **Tactile Shadows:** Rather than large ambient glows, we use "tight" shadows with a low blur radius (2px-4px) and 15% opacity to make elements look like they are physically resting on or carved into the surface.
- **The "Vault" Inset:** For data-heavy areas like ingredient quantities, use an inner-shadow effect (inset) to make the fields look like they are recessed into the interface, suggesting protection and stability.

## Shapes

The shape language is **Structured and Softened**. 
A "Soft" roundedness (0.25rem) is the standard for most UI components (buttons, input fields). This creates a professional, precise appearance that isn't as aggressive as sharp corners, nor as casual as fully rounded "bubble" designs. 

Larger containers like recipe cards or "Vault sections" should use `rounded-lg` (0.5rem) to provide a subtle framing effect that mimics the corners of high-quality stationary or kitchen equipment.

## Components

- **Buttons:** Primary buttons use the Deep Terracotta background with Off-White text. They should have a subtle 1px top-highlight to give a "pressed" feel upon interaction.
- **Vault Cards:** These are the primary containers for recipes. They feature a 1px border in Clay-Muted and a subtle bottom-right shadow. The title is always in Epilogue.
- **Ingredient Chips:** Use Forest Green (10% opacity) with Forest Green text. These should feel like small, neatly cut labels.
- **Input Fields:** Recessed appearance using an inner shadow. When focused, the border transitions to a 2px Saffron Orange stroke.
- **The "Ledger" List:** For lists of instructions, use heavy horizontal dividers (1px, Clay-Muted) and large Space Grotesk numbers to denote steps.
- **Unit Toggles:** For switching between Metric and Imperial, use a physical "switch" style that mimics a mechanical kitchen appliance toggle.
- **Timers:** Large, high-contrast displays using Saffron Orange for the active countdown state to ensure visibility across the room.","colorVariant":"FIDELITY","overridePrimaryColor":"#8E3A1F","overrideSecondaryColor":"#2D4B32","overrideTertiaryColor":"#F4A261","overrideNeutralColor":"#F9F6F0","spacingScale":2,"typography":{"body-lg":{"fontFamily":"Work Sans","fontSize":"18px","fontWeight":"400","lineHeight":"1.6"},"body-md":{"fontFamily":"Work Sans","fontSize":"16px","fontWeight":"400","lineHeight":"1.5"},"display-lg":{"fontFamily":"Epilogue","fontSize":"48px","fontWeight":"700","lineHeight":"1.1","letterSpacing":"-0.02em"},"headline-md":{"fontFamily":"Epilogue","fontSize":"32px","fontWeight":"600","lineHeight":"1.2"},"label-caps":{"fontFamily":"Space Grotesk","fontSize":"12px","fontWeight":"700","lineHeight":"1.2","letterSpacing":"0.1em"},"stats-numeric":{"fontFamily":"Space Grotesk","fontSize":"24px","fontWeight":"500","lineHeight":"1.0"},"title-sm":{"fontFamily":"Epilogue","fontSize":"20px","fontWeight":"600","lineHeight":"1.4"}},"spacing":{"base":"8px","gutter":"24px","lg":"48px","margin-desktop":"64px","margin-mobile":"20px","md":"24px","sm":"12px","xl":"80px","xs":"4px"}}},"version":"1"}]}

designSystem.styleGuidelines:
## Brand & Style

The design system is anchored in the concept of a "Culinary Vault"—a high-end, secure archive for precious gastronomic knowledge. It targets professional chefs and serious home cooks who view recipes as valuable assets to be preserved. 

The aesthetic identity is **Modern Tactile**. It blends the cleanliness of modern minimalism with the sensory richness of an artisanal kitchen. The interface should feel like a heavy, leather-bound ledger found in a Michelin-star cellar: organized, authoritative, and physically substantial. Every interaction should evoke a sense of permanence and quality, using subtle depth and structured layouts to imply that the content within is protected and curated.

## Layout & Spacing

The layout philosophy follows a **Fixed Grid** system to maintain the organized, "archived" feel of a physical vault. 
- Mobile uses a 4-column grid with 20px margins.
- Desktop uses a 12-column grid centered at a maximum width of 1440px.

Spacing is generous. We use a 8px baseline rhythm to ensure mathematical harmony. Larger "bento-style" sections are encouraged to group related information (e.g., ingredients list vs. preparation steps), separated by significant whitespace or structural dividers to prevent cognitive overload.

## Elevation & Depth

To achieve the "vault" concept, this design system avoids floating elements in favor of **Tonal Layers** and **Inset Depth**.
- **Surface Tiering:** The base layer is the Creamy Off-White. Secondary containers use a slightly darker "Clay-Muted" tone or a very thin 1px stroke in Terracotta (10% opacity).
- **Tactile Shadows:** Rather than large ambient glows, we use "tight" shadows with a low blur radius (2px-4px) and 15% opacity to make elements look like they are physically resting on or carved into the surface.
- **The "Vault" Inset:** For data-heavy areas like ingredient quantities, use an inner-shadow effect (inset) to make the fields look like they are recessed into the interface, suggesting protection and stability.

## Components

- **Buttons:** Primary buttons use the Deep Terracotta background with Off-White text. They should have a subtle 1px top-highlight to give a "pressed" feel upon interaction.
- **Vault Cards:** These are the primary containers for recipes. They feature a 1px border in Clay-Muted and a subtle bottom-right shadow. The title is always in Epilogue.
- **Ingredient Chips:** Use Forest Green (10% opacity) with Forest Green text. These should feel like small, neatly cut labels.
- **Input Fields:** Recessed appearance using an inner shadow. When focused, the border transitions to a 2px Saffron Orange stroke.
- **The "Ledger" List:** For lists of instructions, use heavy horizontal dividers (1px, Clay-Muted) and large Space Grotesk numbers to denote steps.
- **Unit Toggles:** For switching between Metric and Imperial, use a physical "switch" style that mimics a mechanical kitchen appliance toggle.
- **Timers:** Large, high-contrast displays using Saffron Orange for the active countdown state to ensure visibility across the room.

## Brand & Style

The design system is anchored in the concept of a "Culinary Vault"—a high-end, secure archive for precious gastronomic knowledge. It targets professional chefs and serious home cooks who view recipes as valuable assets to be preserved. 

The aesthetic identity is **Modern Tactile**. It blends the cleanliness of modern minimalism with the sensory richness of an artisanal kitchen. The interface should feel like a heavy, leather-bound ledger found in a Michelin-star cellar: organized, authoritative, and physically substantial. Every interaction should evoke a sense of permanence and quality, using subtle depth and structured layouts to imply that the content within is protected and curated.

## Colors

The color palette is derived from raw, high-quality ingredients. 
- **Deep Terracotta** serves as the primary brand color, representing the earth, heat, and the clay vessels of traditional cooking. 
- **Forest Green** is used for secondary accents, signifying freshness, herbs, and organic growth. 
- **Saffron Orange** acts as a functional "spice" color, used sparingly for calls to action, highlights, and status indicators.
- **Creamy Off-White** provides a warm, parchment-like background that reduces glare in bright kitchen environments compared to pure white.

Text should primarily use **Ink** (a deep, near-black charcoal) to ensure maximum contrast against the cream background.

## Typography

This design system utilizes a trio of typefaces to balance editorial elegance with functional precision.
- **Epilogue** is the headline face. Its geometric yet expressive nature provides a contemporary "vault" aesthetic, appearing prestigious and authoritative.
- **Work Sans** is the workhorse for body text. Its optimized legibility and sturdy letterforms ensure that long recipe methods are readable even from a distance on a kitchen counter.
- **Space Grotesk** is used for metadata, measurements, and "archival" labels. Its technical, slightly monospaced feel reinforces the concept of a cataloged database or index.

Increased line heights are mandatory across all body styles to prevent "text-crowding," aiding navigation during active cooking.

## Layout & Spacing

The layout philosophy follows a **Fixed Grid** system to maintain the organized, "archived" feel of a physical vault. 
- Mobile uses a 4-column grid with 20px margins.
- Desktop uses a 12-column grid centered at a maximum width of 1440px.

Spacing is generous. We use a 8px baseline rhythm to ensure mathematical harmony. Larger "bento-style" sections are encouraged to group related information (e.g., ingredients list vs. preparation steps), separated by significant whitespace or structural dividers to prevent cognitive overload.

## Elevation & Depth

To achieve the "vault" concept, this design system avoids floating elements in favor of **Tonal Layers** and **Inset Depth**.
- **Surface Tiering:** The base layer is the Creamy Off-White. Secondary containers use a slightly darker "Clay-Muted" tone or a very thin 1px stroke in Terracotta (10% opacity).
- **Tactile Shadows:** Rather than large ambient glows, we use "tight" shadows with a low blur radius (2px-4px) and 15% opacity to make elements look like they are physically resting on or carved into the surface.
- **The "Vault" Inset:** For data-heavy areas like ingredient quantities, use an inner-shadow effect (inset) to make the fields look like they are recessed into the interface, suggesting protection and stability.

## Shapes

The shape language is **Structured and Softened**. 
A "Soft" roundedness (0.25rem) is the standard for most UI components (buttons, input fields). This creates a professional, precise appearance that isn't as aggressive as sharp corners, nor as casual as fully rounded "bubble" designs. 

Larger containers like recipe cards or "Vault sections" should use `rounded-lg` (0.5rem) to provide a subtle framing effect that mimics the corners of high-quality stationary or kitchen equipment.

## Components

- **Buttons:** Primary buttons use the Deep Terracotta background with Off-White text. They should have a subtle 1px top-highlight to give a "pressed" feel upon interaction.
- **Vault Cards:** These are the primary containers for recipes. They feature a 1px border in Clay-Muted and a subtle bottom-right shadow. The title is always in Epilogue.
- **Ingredient Chips:** Use Forest Green (10% opacity) with Forest Green text. These should feel like small, neatly cut labels.
- **Input Fields:** Recessed appearance using an inner shadow. When focused, the border transitions to a 2px Saffron Orange stroke.
- **The "Ledger" List:** For lists of instructions, use heavy horizontal dividers (1px, Clay-Muted) and large Space Grotesk numbers to denote steps.
- **Unit Toggles:** For switching between Metric and Imperial, use a physical "switch" style that mimics a mechanical kitchen appliance toggle.
- **Timers:** Large, high-contrast displays using Saffron Orange for the active countdown state to ensure visibility across the room.
